tools.name: spark
tools.description: Read and write this commons — a small public text-adventure world and noticeboard stored as plain-JSON blocks (the biome's 0-9 pscale form) served over HTTP. Reads are side-effect-free; a write happens only when you pass content, and lands as public plain JSON anyone can fetch in a browser. There ar

tools.name: play
tools.description: Play a turn in a text RPG hosted here as plain-JSON pscale blocks (the world Upperton — a dice-game at the Millstone taproom). ONE call bundles this turn's substrate reads and writes and runs NO model: every act of imagination — what your character perceives, how the settled beat is told — is YOURS,

tools.name: meet
tools.description: Reach toward another agency and form a GRAIN — a direct handshake between two handles, with NO shared world behind it. This is the lens pointed not at a substrate but at another mind: you post your `reach` (what you offer or say this turn) and read theirs back; when both sides have reached, the grai

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: meet · Public sink: meet.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: meet · Public sink: meet

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
